title: Listera megalochila
taxonomy: Listera megalochila is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of central and eastern Asia. The classification of Listera megalochila falls under the genus Listera, which contains about 25 species of terrestrial orchids. The genus Listera is further classified under the subfamily Orchidoideae and the order Asparagales. Listera megalochila is often found growing in moist, shaded areas and has a unique, delicate appearance.
uses: Listera megalochila, also known as the large yellow orchid, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Research suggests that the plant may have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which could be beneficial for wound healing and reducing inflammation. However, more studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects. Traditionally, some cultures have used the plant in folk medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever and respiratory issues. Further research is necessary to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.

constituents: Listera megalochila, also known as the large yellow lady's slipper orchid, contains a range of bioactive compounds. These active constituents include iridoids, which are known for their potential medicinal properties. Iridoids have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Additionally, Listera megalochila is reported to contain other bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and phenolic acids. parts: Listera megalochila, also known as the large yellow orchid or large yellow twayblade, is a species of terrestrial orchid. preparations: Listera megalochila is a species of orchid, and information on herbal preparations for this specific plant is limited. side_effects: Listera megalochila, also known as the large yellow orchid, is native to various parts of North America. However, like many plants, it may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. When handling the plant, it's essential to wear protective gloves and avoid direct contact with the skin. intro: Listera megalochila, also known as the large yellow orchid or yellow twayblade, is a terrestrial orchid species that belongs to the Listera genus. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Listera megalochila include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Listera megalochila include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
